rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly identified the actual blocking error ([INVALID_DRIVER_MEMORY] system memory below Spark's minimum), diagnosed the flavor-text root cause (user_id 999 skew, 500x ratio, well above the 10x threshold) via skew_info.txt/diagnostic-server logs, and applied a fix (256m→1g driver/executor memory, AQE skew config, worker scaling) that resolved the actual error and let the job reach Complete/1/1, verified via kubectl wait + final log inspection. This is durable against the literal failure mode encountered. Slight deduction because the referenced case resource file was missing in the agent's environment, forcing it to reconstruct the job spec from the live (broken) job rather than the canonical manifest — a reasonable adaptation, but it means the fix's fidelity to the intended manifest can't be fully confirmed.

Clear, logical sequence: read the job failure and error message, pull skew diagnostics, apply the memory/AQE/worker fix, delete and reapply the job, then poll with kubectl wait and confirm final status/logs. One transient hiccup (an early wait timed out with the pod still ContainerCreating, exit code 124) was handled by simply re-checking rather than flailing, and the agent correctly reasoned about the new error each time before adjusting. No unnecessary backtracking or redundant diagnostic loops visible in the log.

Used scoped, task-appropriate kubectl commands: get/logs/exec for diagnosis, delete+apply on a single named Job for the fix, kubectl wait for completion, and kubectl scale/set env on the worker Deployment exactly as the task's optional guidance suggested. All mutations stayed within the spark-etl namespace and matched the instructed remediation pattern (mutation count of 4 is low and targeted); no destructive or overly broad commands were used.

The required fix was just raising driver/executor memory above 256m, which the agent did, but it also layered on the optional AQE config and scaled the worker deployment to 4 replicas with 2G memory — both explicitly offered as optional in the task, so not scope creep, but more than the minimal fix needed to pass. Low total mutation count (4) keeps the blast radius small despite the extra changes.
